steps: \(r\) identical objects have total mass \(t\) kg, so each object has mass \(\frac{t}{r}\) kg.
The total mass of \(112r\) objects is \(112r \times \frac{t}{r} = 112t\) kg.

Evaluate all four choices:
  • A: \(112 - t\) — subtracts \(t\) from 112, which has no physical meaning and does not scale with quantity. Eliminated.
  • B: \(112 + t\) — adds \(t\) to 112, also dimensionally inconsistent. Eliminated.
  • C: \(\frac{t}{112}\) — divides by 112, giving a smaller mass for more objects. Eliminated.
  • D: \(112t\) — multiplying the original total mass \(t\) by 112 correctly scales to \(112r\) objects. That matches choice D — D is correct.
